title: Read-only Cubie2/Cubie3 boot-sector firmware string audit
method: Read-only sudo dd from /dev/mmcblk0 first 16 MiB, /dev/mmcblk0p1 first 16 MiB, and /dev/mmcblk0p2 first 64 MiB.
Generated sha256 sums and strings -a -n 6 on Strix.
No board writes, no reboot, no extlinux changes.
findings: The first 16 MiB of each SD card contains real boot-stage firmware strings, correcting H106's metadata-only artifact gap.
Cubie2 and Cubie3 pre-partition captures differ by hash, while their p2 first-64MiB captures match.
Strings include BOOT0, U-Boot, BL31, ARISC/SCP, sdmmc0_mod, sdmmc0_bus, sdmmc0_rst, sdc_mbus, nsi clk update failures, and security_state <= NON_SECURE.
No string hit was found for SMHC, IDMAC, firewall, TZMA, or SPC.
The captured strings confirm vendor boot firmware knows SDMMC/MBUS/NSI names, but do not expose a concrete Linux-programmable SMHC0 IDMAC descriptor-fetch permission bit.
decision: No kernel patch. Preserve the boot-sector captures on Strix and use them for future binary offset/string analysis. Do not infer a DTS or driver change from the generic SDMMC/MBUS/NSI strings alone.
next: If continuing firmware analysis, locate offsets for sdmmc0_mod/sdc_mbus/nsi strings in the pre-partition binary and compare nearby code/data against public U-Boot or vendor symbols before proposing any runtime test.
